metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

evidence: 4 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or extend AC #2's verify step) that asserts the resolved prompt contains the false-positive guard clauses — specifically that silence about portability and inability to construct a counterexample are both documented as must-not-fire conditions.
evidence: Child delivery map: 'immune-floury-toad — owns routing, activation, prompt resolution, leaf/container fan-in, rubric structure, false-positive instructions, and overlay-absent integration coverage'
AC #2: 'proves the contract-bearing project rubric resolves through the production prompt loader' — verify step only runs prompt_resolution test, no false-positive content assertion

The 'What' section defines explicit must-not-fire conditions ('Silence about portability is not a finding' and 'If the reviewer cannot construct the cited, concrete counterexample, it emits no finding') that should be reflected in the prompt
findings.finding: The child delivery map explicitly assigns `immune-floury-toad` ownership of 'false-positive instructions' as a distinct prompt deliverable, but no AC verifies that the resolved prompt contains false-positive guard content. AC #2 only proves the prompt *resolves* through the production loader; it does not verify the presence or correctness of the false-positive instructions that govern when the criterion must stay silent.
findings.location: Child delivery map (`immune-floury-toad` entry) / AC #2 (prompt resolution)
scenarios: Prompt resolves correctly (AC #2 passes) but omits the false-positive suppression instructions; the live criterion fires on silent plans, causing the live eval AC #7 to fail — but by that point the blast radius spans two children.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC #2: 'proves the contract-bearing project rubric resolves through the production prompt loader AND the resolved text contains the must-not-fire clauses for silence and absent counterexample.' Update the verify step to include `-k 'prompt_resolution or false_positive_guard'`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile 'all four concrete portability failures' in Success Criteria with AC #7 by either (a) explicitly naming the four required must-fire cases in the AC or a referenced corpus definition, or (b) rewording the Success Criteria to 'at least one must-fire case per matrix dimension' and tying that count to the eval AC's corpus spec.
evidence: Success Criteria: 'A balanced three-run evaluation demonstrates all four concrete portability failures'
AC #7: 'records three live eval runs with recall 1.0, false-accept rate 0.0, per-case stability of at least two of three, and Cohen kappa of at least 0.70' — no case-count constraint
Counterexample matrix defines four category dimensions, not four individually enumerable cases
findings.finding: The Success Criteria says "demonstrates all four concrete portability failures" (implying exactly four must-fire eval cases, one per matrix dimension), but AC #7 only requires recall=1.0 and Cohen kappa≥0.70 against whatever corpus `indigoid-unmystic-serpent` defines — it never anchors the must-fire case count to four. The matrix lists four *categories* (harness, target project, platform/venue, project location/access), not four enumerated cases. An implementer could create 2 or 8 must-fire cases and satisfy AC #7 while failing the Success Criteria's 'all four' claim, or vice versa.
findings.location: Success Criteria paragraph / AC #7 (`indigoid-unmystic-serpent` eval run)
scenarios: Implementer creates two must-fire cases (one each for harness and target-project dimensions), achieves recall=1.0 on those two, but does not cover platform/venue or project-location — passes AC #7, fails Success Criteria intent.
Implementer creates eight cases across four dimensions, satisfies 'all four', but AC #7 doesn't require this.
findings.suggested_fix: In AC #7 add a parenthetical such as '(corpus must include at least one must-fire case per counterexample-matrix dimension: harness, target project, platform/venue, and project location/access)' so the count of four is verifiable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the default calibration policy in the `indigoid-unmystic-serpent` section: specify what triggers a rubric edit (e.g., any threshold miss on a three-run batch), what categories of change are permitted (instructions, examples, contrast cases), and what the default action is when all thresholds are already met on the first run.
evidence: 'any calibration-driven edits confined to the project rubric' — no trigger condition, permitted edit types, or default (no-edit) case is stated
The corresponding AC (`[operator-attested]`) attests the eval results but does not specify what the child must do when results fall short of thresholds
findings.finding: The scope of 'calibration-driven edits' delegated to `indigoid-unmystic-serpent` is unspecified: the plan says the child owns 'any calibration-driven edits confined to the project rubric' but does not state what conditions trigger an edit, what kinds of changes are permitted (rewording instructions, adding/removing examples, tightening thresholds), or what the default action is if the three-run thresholds are already met. An executing agent must invent this policy.
findings.location: Child delivery map — `indigoid-unmystic-serpent` bullet; also Scope section 'Focused positive, negative, leaf, container, routing, and rendering tests'
scenarios: An agent executing `indigoid-unmystic-serpent` achieves recall 0.8 on the first run; the plan gives no instruction on whether to reword the prompt, add counterexamples, tighten the four-part contract wording, or escalate — the agent must guess.
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the `indigoid-unmystic-serpent` bullet: 'If any threshold (recall, FAR, kappa) is missed on a run, the child may reword finder instructions or add/remove contrast examples in the project rubric prompt; structural schema changes (criterion id, activation params) are out of scope for this child. If all thresholds pass on the first run, no rubric edits are made.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the operative role of the `gold_set` in the eval spec: either (a) add an LLM-judge scorer that consumes it and align the label vocabulary (`useful`/`not_useful`) to the existing judge contract, or (b) remove the gold_set requirement from the AC and Approach if `calibrate_criterion`'s dataset-based kappa is the sole release gate.
evidence: eval.py:265-271 shows gold_set is validated and consumed exclusively for LLM-judge kappa alignment.
The child plan says 'requires no LLM-judge scorer' and declares only `emits_valid_findings` (deterministic).
validate_dataset_and_gold enforces `gold_set[i]` has `input` + `label`, but does not validate label vocabulary against `finding`/`pass` vs `useful`/`not_useful`.
The gold_set's `finding`/`pass` label vocabulary differs from all existing gold_sets which use `useful`/`not_useful` for LLM-judge grading.
findings.finding: The eval spec for `indigoid-unmystic-serpent` requires a `gold_set` with `finding`/`pass` labels but declares only a single `emits_valid_findings` deterministic scorer and explicitly disavows an LLM-judge scorer. In the existing system, `gold_set` is consumed exclusively by LLM-judge kappa alignment (`eval.py:265-271`); `calibrate_criterion` never reads `gold_set` — it reads `dataset` entries only. The `gold_set`'s `finding`/`pass` label values therefore have no specified consumer or processing protocol anywhere in the plan or the codebase. An implementer who runs `validate_eval_spec(spec, strict=True)` will satisfy the structural check (non-empty gold_set with input+label), but the labels serve no operative purpose and the plan provides no instruction for how the gold_set drives anything in this eval.
findings.location: Child ticket `indigoid-unmystic-serpent` (21dd-8681-4447-4bc0), Approach section: 'a balanced `dataset` plus a non-empty `gold_set`' and 'Kappa is specifically `calibrate_criterion` expected-vs-observed fire/no-fire agreement over the eight dataset cases; it is not `judge_alignment` and requires no LLM-judge scorer.'
scenarios: An implementer authors the gold_set correctly per the plan but nothing reads it during calibration — the kappa metric the plan gates on comes from `calibrate_criterion`'s dataset, not from the gold_set, so the gold_set is structurally present but semantically inert.

If a future consumer (e.g. an LLM-judge scorer added later) reads the gold_set, it will find `finding`/`pass` labels rather than the `useful`/`not_useful` vocabulary used by all existing gold_sets, causing a silent mismatch.
findings.suggested_fix: If the gold_set is intended as a structural completeness marker only (satisfying `validate_eval_spec strict=True`), update the child plan to state this explicitly and align the label vocabulary to `useful`/`not_useful` to match the judge contract. If it is not needed, drop it from the AC and Approach to avoid a misspecified schema with no consumer.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
